author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: David the king was one man, but not one man he figured; sometimes to wit he figured the Church of many men consisting, extended even unto the ends of the earth: but sometimes One Man he figured, Him he figured that is Mediator of God and men, the Man Christ Jesus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this Psalm therefore, or rather in this Psalm’s title, certain victorious actions of David are spoken of:…“To the end, in behalf of those men that shall be changed unto the title’s inscription, unto teaching for David himself, when he burned up Mesopotamia in Syria, and Syria Sobal, and turned Joab, and smote Edom, in the valley of salt-pits twelve thousand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We read of these things in the books of the Reigns, that all those persons whom he hath named, were defeated by David, that is, Mesopotamia in Syria, and Syria Sobal, Joab, Edom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things were done, and just as they were done, so there they have been written, so they are read: let him read that will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, as the Prophetic Spirit in the Psalms’ titles is wont to depart somewhat from the expression of things done, and to say something which in history is not found, and hence rather to admonish us that titles of this kind have been written not that we may know things done, but that things future may be prefigured.…But here this thing is inserted for this especial reason, that there it is not written that he burned up Mesopotamia in Syria, and Syria Sobal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now let us begin to examine these things after the significations of things future, and to bring out the dimness of shadows into the light of the word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “to the end” ye know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the end of the law is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those that are changed ye know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who but they that do pass from old life into new?…“For ye were sometime darkness, but now light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they are changed “into the title’s inscription,”…who into the kingdom of Christ do pass over from the kingdom of the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is well that they are changed unto this title’s inscription.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they are changed, as followeth, “unto teaching.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He added, “for David himself unto teaching:” that is, are changed not for themselves, but for David himself, and are changed unto teaching.…When therefore would Christ have changed us, unless He had done that which He spake of, “Fire I have come to send into the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore Christ came to send into the world fire, to wit to its health and profit, we must inquire not how He is to send the world into fire, but how into the world fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Inasmuch as therefore He came to send fire into the world, let us inquire what is Mesopotamia which was burned up, what is Syria Sobal?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The interpretations therefore of the names let us examine according to the Hebrew language, wherein first this Scripture was written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mesopotamia they say is interpreted, “exalted calling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the whole world by calling hath been exalted, Syria is interpreted “lofty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But she which was lofty, burned up hath been and humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sobal is interpreted “empty antiquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks to Christ that hath burned her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whenever old bushes are burned up, green places succeed; and more speedily and more plentifully, and more fully green, fresh ones spring out, when fire hath gone before them to the burning up of the old.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not therefore the fire of Christ be feared, hay it consumeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For all flesh is hay, and all the glory of man as flower of hay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He burneth up therefore those things with that fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And turned Joab.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Joab is interpreted enemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was turned an enemy, as thou wilt understand it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If turned unto flight, the devil it is: if converted to the faith, a Christian it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How unto flight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the heart of a Christian: “The Prince of this world,” He saith, “now hath been cast out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how can a Christian turned to the Lord be an enemy turned?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he hath become a believer that had been an enemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Smote Edom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Edom is interpreted “earthly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That earthly one ought to be smitten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For why should one live earthly, that ought to live heavenly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hath been slain therefore life earthly, let there live life heavenly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For as we have borne the image of the earthly, let us bear also the image of Him that is from Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See it slain: “Mortify your members which are upon earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when he had smitten Edom, he smote “twelve thousand in the valley of salt-pits.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Twelve thousand is a perfect number, to which perfect number also the number of the twelve Apostles is ascribed: for not to no purpose is it, but because through the whole world was to be sent the Word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Word of God, which is Christ, is in clouds, that is, in the preachers of truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the world of four parts doth consist.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The four parts thereof are exceeding well known to all, and often in the Scriptures they are mentioned: they are the same as the name of the four winds, East, West, North, and South.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all these four parts was sent the Word, so that in the Trinity all might be called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The number twelve four times three do make.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason therefore twelve thousand earthly things were smitten, the whole world was smitten: for from the whole world was chosen out the Church, mortified from earthly life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why “in the valley of salt-pits”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A valley is humility: salt-pits signify savour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many men are humbled, but emptily and foolishly, in empty oldness they are humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One suffereth tribulation for money, suffereth tribulation for temporal honour, suffereth tribulation for the comforts of this life; he is to suffer tribulation and to be humbled: why not for the sake of God? why not for the sake of Christ? why not for the savour of salt?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Knowest thou not that to thee hath been said, “Ye are the salt of earth,” and, “If the salt shall have been spoiled, for no other thing will it be of use, but to be cast out”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A good thing it is therefore wisely to be humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold now are not heretics being humbled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not laws been made even by men to condemn them, against whom divine laws do reign, which even before had condemned them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold they are humbled, behold they are put to flight, behold persecution they suffer, but without savour; for folly, for emptiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now the salt hath been spoiled: therefore it hath been cast out, to be trodden down of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have heard the title of the Psalm, let us hear also the words of the Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God, Thou hast driven us back, and hast destroyed us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is that David speaking that smote, that burned up, that defeated, and not they to whom He did these things, that is to say, their being smitten and driven back, that were evil men, and again their being made alive and returning in order that they might be good men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That destruction indeed that David made, strong of hand, our Christ, whose figure that man was bearing; He did those things, He made this destruction with His sword and with His fire: for both He brought into this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both “Fire I am come to send into the world,” thou hast in the Gospel: and “A sword I have come to send into the earth,” thou hast in the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He brought in fire, whereby might be burned up Mesopotamia in Syria, and Syria Sobal: He brought in a sword whereby might be smitten Edom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now again this destruction was made for the sake of “those that are changed unto the title’s inscription.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear we therefore the voice of them: to their health smitten they were, being raised up let them speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them say, therefore, that are changed into something better, changed unto the title’s inscription, changed unto teaching for David himself; let them say, “Thou hast had mercy upon us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast destroyed us, in order that Thou mightest build us; Thou hast destroyed us that were ill builded, hast destroyed empty oldness; in order that there may be a building unto a new man, building to abide for everlasting.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast moved the earth, and hast troubled it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How hath the earth been troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the conscience of sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither go we?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither flee we, when this sword hath been brandished, “Repent, for near hath drawn the kingdom of Heaven”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Heal the crushings thereof, for moved it hath been.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unworthy it is to be healed, if moved it hath not been: but thou speakest, preachest, threatenest us with God, of coming judgment holdest not thy peace, of the commandment of God thou warnest, from these things thou abstainest not; and he that heareth, if he feareth not, if he is not moved, is not worthy to be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another heareth, is moved, is stung, smiteth the breast, sheddeth tears.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The first labour is, that thou shouldest be displeasing to thyself, that sins thou shouldest battle out, that thou shouldest be changed into something better: the second labour, in return for thy having been changed, is to bear the tribulations and temptations of this world, and amid them to hold on even unto the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of these things therefore when he was speaking, while pointing out such things, he addeth what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast shown to Thy people hard things” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): to Thy people now, made tributary after the victory of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast shown to Thy people hard things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In persecutions which the Church of Christ hath endured, when so much blood of martyrs was spilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast given us to drink of the wine of goading.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of goading” is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not of killing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was not a killing that destroyeth, but a medicine that smarteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast given us to drink of the wine of goading.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast given to men fearing Thee, a sign that they should flee from the face of the bow” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through tribulations temporal, he saith, Thou hast signified to Thine own to flee from the wrath of fire everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, saith the Apostle Peter, “Time it is that Judgment begin with the House of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And exhorting the Martyrs to endurance, when the world should rage, when slaughters should be made at the hands of persecutors, when far and wide blood of believers should be spilled, when in chains, in prisons, in tortures, many hard things Christians should suffer, in these hard things, I say, lest they should faint, Peter saith to them, “Time it is that Judgment begin with the House of God,” etc. What therefore is to be in the Judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bow is bended, still in menacing posture it is, not yet in aiming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And see what there is in the bow: is there not an arrow to be shot forward?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The string however is stretched back in a contrary direction to that in which it is going to be shot; and the more the stretching thereof hath gone backward, with the greater swiftness it starteth forward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that I have said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The more the Judgment is deferred, with so much the greater swiftness it is to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore even for temporal tribulations to God let us render thanks, because He hath given to His people a sign, “that they should flee from the face of the bow:” in order that His faithful ones having been exercised in tribulations temporal, may be worthy to avoid the condemnation of fire everlasting, which is to find out all them that do not believe these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That Thy beloved may be delivered: save me with Thy right hand, and hearken unto me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With Thy right hand save me, Lord: so save me as that at the right hand I may stand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any safety temporal I require, in this matter Thy Will be done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a time what is good for us we are utterly ignorant: for “what we should pray for as we ought we know not:” but “save me with Thy right hand,” so that even if in this time I suffer sundry tribulations, when the night of all tribulations hath been spent, on the right hand I may be found among the sheep, not on the left hand among the goats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hearken unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because now I am deserving that which Thou art willing to give; not “with the words of my transgressions” I am crying through the day, so that Thou hearken not, and “in the night so that Thou hearken not,” and that not for folly to me,” but truly for my warning, by adding savour from the valley of salt-pits, so that in tribulation I may know what to ask: but I ask life everlasting; therefore hearken unto me, because Thy right hand I ask.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God hath spoken in His Holy One” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6).…In what Holy One of His?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that Holy One, of whom elsewhere ye have heard, “O God, in the Holy One is Thy way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will rejoice and will divide Sichima.…and the valley of tabernacles I will measure out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sichima is interpreted shoulders.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But according to history, Jacob returning from Laban his father-in-law with all his kindred, hid the idols in Sichima which he had from Syria, where for a long time he had dwelled, and at length was coming from thence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But tabernacles he made there because of his sheep and herds, and called the place Tabernacles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these I will divide, saith the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, “I will divide Sichima”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If to the story where the idols were hidden is the reference, the Gentiles it signifieth; I divide the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I divide, is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For not in all men is there faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I divide, is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some will believe, others will not believe.…The shoulders are divided, in order that their sins may burthen some men, while others may take up the burden of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For godly shoulders He was requiring when He said, “For My yoke is gentle, and My burden is light.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another burden oppresseth and loadeth thee, but Christ’s burden relieveth thee: another burden hath weight, Christ’s burden hath wings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even if thou pull off the wings from a bird, thou dost remove a kind of weight; and the more weight thou hast taken away, the more on earth it will abide.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She that thou hast chosen to disburden lieth there: she flieth not, because thou hast taken off a weight: let there be given back the weight, and she flieth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is Christ’s burden; let men carry it, and not be idle: let them not be heeded that will not bear it; let them bear it that will, and they shall find how light it is, how sweet, how pleasant, how ravishing unto Heaven, and from earth how transporting.…Perchance because of the sheep of Jacob, “the valley of Tabernacles” is to be understood of the nation of the Jews, and the same is divided: for they have passed from thence that have believed, the rest have remained without.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine is Galaad” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These names are read in the Scriptures of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Galaad hath the voice of an interpretation of its own and of a great Mystery: for it is interpreted “the heap of testimony.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great a heap of testimony in the Martyrs?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine is Galaad,” mine is a heap of testimony, mine are the true Martyrs.…Then meanly esteemed was the Church among men, then reproach on Her a Widow was being thrown, because Christ’s She was, because the sign of the Cross on her brow She was wearing: not yet was there honour, censure there was then: when therefore not honour, but censure there was, then was made a heap of witness; and through the heap of witness was the Love of Christ enlarged; and through the enlargement of the Love of Christ, were the Gentiles possessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There followeth, “And mine is Manasses;” which is interpreted forgotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to Her had been said, “Confusion for everlasting Thou shalt forget, and of the reproach of Thy widowhood Thou shalt not be mindful.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was therefore a confusion of the Church once, which now hath been forgotten: for of Her confusion and of the “reproach” of Her widowhood now She is not mindful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when there was a sort of confusion among men, a heap of witness was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now no longer doth any even remember that confusion, when it was a reproach to be a Christian, now no one remembereth, now all have forgotten, now “Mine is Manasses, and Ephraim the strength of My head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ephraim is interpreted fruitfulness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mine, he saith, is fruitfulness, and this fruitfulness is the strength of My Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For My Head is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence is fruitfulness the strength of Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because unless a grain were to fall into the earth, it would not be multiplied, alone it would remain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fall then to earth did Christ in His Passion, and there followed fruit-bearing in the Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was hanging and was being despised: the grain was within, it had powers to draw after it all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How in a grain do numbers of seeds lie hid, something abject it appeareth to the eyes, but a power turning into itself matter and bringing forth fruit is hidden; so in Christ’s Cross virtue was hidden, there appeared weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O mighty grain!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doubtless weak is He that hangeth, Doubtless before Him that people did wag the head, Doubtless they said, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the strength of Him: that which is a weak thing of God, is stronger than men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason so great fruitfulness hath followed: it is mine, saith the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Juda is my king: Moab the pot of my hope” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that is of the tribe of Juda.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What Juda, but He to whom Jacob himself said, “Juda, thy brethren shall praise thee”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore should I fear, when Juda my king saith, “Fear not them that kill the body”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Moab the pot of my hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore “pot”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore “of my hope”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there hath gone before Juda my king.…Moab is perceived in the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that nation was born of sin, that nation was born of the daughters of Lot, who lay with their father drunken, abusing a father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Better were it to have remained barren, than thus to have become mothers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this was a kind of figure of them that abuse the law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For do not heed that law in the Latin language is of the feminine gender: in Greek of the masculine gender it is: but whether it be of the feminine gender in speaking, or of the masculine, the expression maketh no difference to the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For law hath rather a masculine force, because it ruleth, is not ruled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But moreover, the Apostle Paul saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Good is the law, if any one use it lawfully.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those daughters of Lot unlawfully used their father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the same manner as good works begin to grow when a man useth well the law: so arise evil works, when a man ill useth the law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, they ill using their father, that is, ill using the law, engendered the Moabites, by whom are signified evil works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence the tribulation of the Church, thence the pot boiling up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this pot in a certain place of prophecy is said, “A pot heated by the North wind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence but by the quarters of the devil, who hath said, “I will set my seat at the North”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The chiefest tribulations therefore arise against the Church from none except from those that ill use the law.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Into Idumæa I will stretch out my shoe” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church speaketh, “I will come through even unto Idumæa.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let tribulations rage, let the world boil with offences, even unto those very persons that lead an earthly life (for Idumæa is interpreted earthly), even unto those same, “even unto Idumæa, I will stretch out my shoe.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what thing the shoe except of the Gospel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How beautiful the feet of them that tell of peace, that tell of good things,” and “the feet shod unto the preparation of the Gospel of peace.”…In these times we see, brethren, how many earthly men do perpetrate frauds for the sake of gain, for frauds perjuries; on account of their fears they consult fortune-tellers, astrologers: all these men are Edomites, earthly; and nevertheless all these men adore Christ, under His own shoe they are; now even unto Idumæa is stretched out His shoe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To Me Allophyli have been made subject.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are “Allophyli”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men of other race, not belonging to My race.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They “have been made subject,” because many men adore Christ, and are not to reign with Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who will lead Me down into the city of standing round?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the city of standing round?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye remember already, I have made mention thereof in another Psalm, wherein hath been said, “And they shall go around the city.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the city of standing round is the compassing around of the Gentiles, which compassing around of the Gentiles in the middle thereof had the one nation of the Jews, worshipping one God: the rest of the compassing around of the Gentiles to idols made supplication, demons they did serve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And mystically it was called the city of standing round; because on all sides the Gentiles had poured themselves around, and had stood around that nation which did worship one God.…“Who will lead me down even unto Idumæa?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wilt not Thou, O God, that hast driven us back?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wilt not Thou, O God, march forth in our powers?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt not Thou lead us down, that hast driven us back?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore “hast driven us back”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Thou hast destroyed us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore hast destroyed us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because angry Thou hast been, and hast had pity on us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou therefore wilt lead down, that hast driven back; Thou, O God, that wilt not march forth in our powers, wilt lead down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “wilt not march forth in our powers”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The world is to rage, the world is to tread us down, there is to be a heap of witnesses, builded of the spilled blood of martyrs, and the raging heathen are to say, “Where is the God of them?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then “Thou wilt not march forth in our powers:” against them Thou wilt not show Thyself, Thou wilt not show Thy power, such as Thou hast shown in David, in Moses, in Joshua the son of Nun, when to their might the Gentiles yielded, and when the slaughter had been ended, and the great laying waste repaired, into the land which Thou promisedst Thou leddest in Thy people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing then Thou wilt not do, “Thou wilt not march forth in our powers,” but within Thou wilt work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “wilt not march forth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt not show Thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For indeed when in chains the Martyrs were being led along, when they were being shut up in prison, when they were being led forth to be mocked, when to the beasts they were exposed, when they were being smitten with the sword, when with fire they were being burned, were they not despised as though forsaken, as though without helper?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner was God working within? in what manner within was He comforting? in what manner to these men was He making sweet the hope of life everlasting? in what manner was He not forsaking the hearts of them, where the man was dwelling in silence, well if good, ill if evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He then by any means forsaking, because He was not marching forth in the powers of them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By not marching forth in the powers of them, did He not the more lead down the Church even unto Idumæa, lead down the Church even unto the city of standing around?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if the Church chose to war and to use the sword, She would seem to be fighting for life present: but because she was despising life present, therefore there was made a heap of witness for the life that shall be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou therefore, O God, that wilt not march forth in our powers, “Give to us aid from tribulation, and vain is the safety of man” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Go now they that salt have not, and desire safety temporal for their friends, which is empty oldness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Give to us aid:” from thence whence Thou wast supposed to forsake, thence succour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In God we will do valour, and Himself to nothing shall bring down our enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will not do valour with the sword, not with horses, not with breastplates, not with shields, not in the mightiness of an army, not abroad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Within, where we are not seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where within?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In God we will do virtue:” and as if abjects, and as if trodden down, men as if of no consideration we shall be, but “Himself to nothing shall bring down our enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a word, this thing hath been done to our enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trodden down have been the Martyrs: by suffering, by enduring, by persevering even unto the end, in God they have done valour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself also hath done that which followeth: to nothing He hath brought down the enemies of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where are now the enemies of the Martyrs, except perchance that now drunken men with their cups do persecute those whom at that time frenzied men did use with stones to persecute?
